sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Understanding the Setup: (WLDR) and Scalable Risk
feed.summary: This article analyzes Affinity World Leaders Equity Etf (NASDAQ: WLDR), indicating a near-term neutral sentiment despite mid and long-term strength, with a mid-channel oscillation pattern. It highlights an exceptional 12.3:1 risk-reward setup targeting a 3.6% gain versus 0.3% risk. The piece details three AI-generated trading strategies—Position, Momentum Breakout, and Risk Hedging—tailored for various risk profiles and holding periods, alongside multi-timeframe signal analysis.

feed.title: 1ST SOURCE CORP SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: 1st Source Corporation (SRCE) has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, showcasing robust financial performance with net income of $158.28 million and diluted net income per common share of $6.41. The company highlighted strategic growth in renewable energy and maintained a strong liquidity position, increasing shareholders' equity to 14.08% of total assets. The report also detailed various challenges and risks, including credit, market, climate change, and regulatory issues.

feed.title: Avis Budget shares tumble 14% as EV fleet challenges drive Q4 loss, revenue miss
feed.summary: Avis Budget Group (NASDAQ:CAR) shares fell 14% after the company reported a Q4 revenue miss and a significant loss, largely due to $518 million in impairment charges related to its U.S. electric vehicle fleet. Despite these challenges, the company's CEO, Brian Choi, stated that Avis is focusing on fleet discipline and strengthening its balance sheet for sustainable earnings growth in 2026. The Q4 revenue was $2.7 billion against an expected $2.75 billion, and the company reported a loss of -$21.25 per share.

feed.title: Dr Reddy's acquires India trademarks for 2 HRT drugs from UK's Mercury Pharma for $32 million
feed.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories is entering the hormone replacement therapy (HRT) segment in India by acquiring trademarks for Progynova and Cyclo-Progynova from Mercury Pharma Group for $32.15 million. This strategic move aims to strengthen Dr Reddy's gynecology portfolio and expand its presence in the HRT market, which addresses symptoms related to estrogen deficiency and menopause. The acquired brands generated sales of Rs 100 crore as per IQVIA MAT December 2025 data.

feed.title: CoStar Group cuts jobs amid AI rollout and Homes.com focus
feed.summary: CoStar Group has confirmed layoffs, attributing them to efficiencies gained from AI adoption and a strategic focus on Homes.com. The company is facing pressure from activist investors to divest Homes.com due to slower-than-expected financial results but remains committed to its long-term growth and digital ecosystem strategy, forecasting profitability for Homes.com by 2030.

feed.title: EBay Reports Quarterly Sales, Profit That Exceeded Estimates
feed.summary: EBay Inc. announced quarterly sales and profit that surpassed analyst estimates. The company's focus on luxury items and refurbished goods is credited with helping it thrive in a competitive e-commerce landscape. For the fourth quarter, eBay reported an adjusted profit of $1.41 per share on $3 billion in revenue, exceeding average estimates of $1.35 per share and $2.87 billion, respectively.

feed.title: BeFra Announces Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release Date, Conference Call and Webcast
feed.summary: Betterware de México, S.A.P.I. de C.V. (BeFra) has announced that it will release its fourth quarter 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 26, 2026, after the U.S. market close. The company will host a conference call and webcast to discuss the results on the same day at 5:30 p.m. (Eastern Time), with replay options available for those unable to participate live. BeFra, also known for its JAFRA acquisition, is a leading direct-to-consumer company in Mexico and the U.S.

feed.title: MetLife Executive to Speak at Raymond James 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference
feed.summary: MetLife, Inc. announced that Ramy Tadros, regional president of MetLife’s U.S. Business and head of MetLife Holdings, will speak at the Raymond James 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference on March 4, 2026, at 10:25 a.m. ET. A live webcast of his presentation will be available online, with a replay accessible until March 11, 2026. This announcement highlights MetLife's engagement with the investment community alongside recent company news regarding employee retention and preferred stock dividends.

feed.title: Invitation Homes Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. announced its Q4 and Full Year 2025 financial results, showing increased revenues and net income. The company highlighted strategic acquisitions of newly-constructed homes, particularly through builder relationships, and the recent acquisition of ResiBuilt Homes to enhance development capabilities. Invitation Homes also provided its FY 2026 guidance, forecasting continued growth in Core FFO and AFFO per share.

feed.title: IQVIA to Speak at Citi's 2026 Unplugged MedTech and Life Sciences Access Day
feed.summary: IQVIA Holdings Inc. announced that CFO Ron Bruehlman and incoming CFO Mike Fedock will speak at Citi's 2026 Unplugged MedTech and Life Sciences Access Day on February 26, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET. A live audio webcast will be available on the IQVIA Investor Relations website, with a replay available later that day. The article also provides details about IQVIA’s services as a global provider of clinical research and healthcare intelligence.

feed.title: EOG COO to present at Raymond James investor conference on March 3
feed.summary: EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) will present at the Raymond James 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference on Tuesday, March 3, 2026, at 7:05 a.m. Central (8:05 a.m. Eastern). Jeffrey R. Leitzell, Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer, will represent EOG at the conference. Live webcasts and replays of the presentation will be accessible on the company's Investors/Events & Presentations page for up to one year.

feed.title: Edison International, Southern California Edison Declare Dividends
feed.summary: Edison International's board of directors declared a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.8775 per share, payable on April 30, 2026, and semiannual dividends for its Series A and Series B preferred stocks. Additionally, Southern California Edison's board declared quarterly dividends for its Series G, K, L, M, and N preference stocks, all payable on March 15, 2026. The announcement details the amounts and record dates for each dividend.

feed.title: Etsy stock pops 18% on sale of Depop to eBay for $1.2 billion
feed.summary: Etsy's stock surged over 17% after announcing the sale of its secondhand clothing marketplace, Depop, to eBay for approximately $1.2 billion in cash. Etsy had originally acquired Depop for about $1.62 billion five years prior, aiming to tap into younger consumers. This divestiture allows Etsy to refocus on its core marketplace growth.

feed.title: Booking Holdings says 25-to-1 stock split to be effected on April 2
feed.summary: Booking Holdings announced a 25-to-1 stock split of its common stock, to be effective on April 2, 2026. This news comes after the company repurchased $2.1 billion of stock in Q4 2025, with a remaining authorization of $21.8 billion. The company reported strong Q4 adjusted EPS and expects Q1 revenue growth of 14%-16%.

feed.title: EBay forecasts first-quarter revenue above estimates
feed.summary: EBay Inc. has forecasted first-quarter revenue exceeding Wall Street estimates, driven by its strategic focus on specialty categories such as luxury goods and auto parts within a challenging e-commerce landscape. The company anticipates revenue between $3 billion and $3.05 billion, surpassing the average analyst estimate of $2.80 billion. Additionally, eBay announced its acquisition of the consumer-to-consumer fashion marketplace Depop from Etsy for nearly $1.2 billion in cash.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (EBAY) eBay Inc. Reports Q4 Revenue $2.96B, vs. FactSet Est of $2.88B
feed.summary: eBay Inc. (EBAY) announced its Q4 revenue reached $2.96 billion, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $2.88 billion. The company also reported an adjusted EPS of $1.41 per share for Q4 and provided Q1 guidance, expecting adjusted EPS between $1.53 and $1.59, with revenue ranging from $3.00 billion to $3.05 billion.

feed.title: Devon Energy (DVN) Reports Strong Q4 and Strategic Merger Plans
feed.summary: Devon Energy (DVN) has announced a strategic merger with Coterra Energy, focusing on the Delaware Basin to drive future growth and operational efficiencies, projecting $1 billion in annual pretax synergies by 2027. The company delivered strong Q4 financial results, surpassing expectations in production and cost metrics, generating $700 million in free cash flow, and achieving a 193% reserve replacement rate. Devon Energy's financial health is robust with strong revenue growth and profitability, though its Altman Z-Score indicates some financial stress, and market sentiment remains positive with a "Buy" consensus from analysts.

feed.title: Broadstone Net Lease Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Broadstone Net Lease, Inc. (BNL) announced its operating results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, highlighting consistent execution and a return to growth. The company reported a 4.2% increase in Adjusted Funds from Operations (AFFO) per diluted share for the full year and achieved 2.0% same store rental revenue growth. BNL also provided its 2026 guidance, expecting AFFO of $1.53 to $1.57 per diluted share, and detailed its investment, disposition, and capital markets activities.

feed.title: Edison International Reports Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Edison International reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2025, exceeding previous year's figures in both GAAP and core EPS. The company also provided optimistic core EPS guidance for 2026 and 2027, citing regulatory decisions and continued confidence in sustained growth from 2025-2030. CEO Pedro J. Pizarro highlighted progress in wildfire mitigation and rate decreases for customers in California.

feed.title: Edison Is Slashing Top Executive Bonuses After Deadly LA Fire
feed.summary: Edison International is reducing top executive bonuses by 40% following a devastating Los Angeles-area wildfire that resulted in 19 deaths and extensive property damage. The company's Southern California utility is being investigated for its potential role in the Eaton Fire, which occurred in January 2025, and is facing numerous lawsuits. This bonus cut reflects the significant impact of the incident on the company.

feed.title: 12 Health Care Stocks Moving In Wednesday's After-Market Session
feed.summary: This article lists 12 healthcare stocks that experienced significant movement in Wednesday's after-market session. Six stocks, including Cardio Diagnostics Holdings and Nakamoto, saw gains, while six others, such as Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals and Aprea Therapeutics, experienced declines. The movements reflect varied market reactions for companies within the health care sector.

feed.title: Avis Budget Group Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year Results
feed.summary: Avis Budget Group (NASDAQ: CAR) announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025. The company reported fourth-quarter revenues of $2.7 billion and a net loss of $856 million, primarily due to a $518 million charge related to shortening the useful life of certain U.S. electric vehicle rental cars. For the full year, Avis Budget Group recorded revenues of $11.7 billion and a net loss of $995 million, with CEO Brian Choi emphasizing a strategic repositioning for sustainable earnings growth in 2026 through fleet discipline, balance sheet strengthening, and improved customer experience.

feed.title: Coherent Corp. to Host a Technology Innovation Briefing on March 17, 2026
feed.summary: Coherent Corp. (NYSE: COHR) announced it will host a Technology Innovation Briefing during the 2026 Optical Fiber Communication Conference and Exhibition (OFC) in Los Angeles on March 17, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. ET. The briefing will include presentations and a Q&A session with key Coherent executives and will be available in-person and via live webcast, with advance registration required.

feed.title: Nurse sues HCA Florida Palms West Hospital, security company 1 year after brutal patient attack
feed.summary: A nurse, Leelamma Lal, has filed a lawsuit against HCA Florida Palms West Hospital and Allied Universal Security one year after a brutal attack by a patient left her with life-altering injuries. The lawsuit alleges negligence in providing adequate security, citing a history of violence at the hospital and a flawed security contract. Lal and her family seek justice and accountability, emphasizing the need for healthcare worker safety and calling for changes beyond financial compensation.

feed.title: Etsy Sells Depop, a Secondhand Clothing App Popular With Gen Z, to eBay
feed.summary: Etsy has sold Depop, a secondhand clothing app popular with Gen Z, to eBay for $1.2 billion. Etsy initially acquired Depop in 2021 for $1.6 billion but is now divesting to refocus on its core marketplace business amidst decelerating growth. For eBay, this acquisition is a strategic move to attract younger users and capitalize on the fast-growing secondhand apparel market.

feed.title: eBay to Acquire Depop from Etsy
feed.summary: eBay Inc. has announced its acquisition of Depop from Etsy for approximately $1.2 billion in cash. This strategic move aims to enhance eBay's consumer-to-consumer (C2C) offerings, particularly by reaching younger, fashion-forward consumers and expanding its presence in the recommerce market. Etsy will use the proceeds to focus on its core marketplace and for general corporate purposes, including share repurchases.

feed.title: BAUSCH HEALTH ANNOUNCES FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL-YEAR 2025 RESULTS
feed.summary: Bausch Health announced strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results, with consolidated revenues up 9% and 7% respectively. The company reported its eleventh consecutive quarter of year-over-year revenue and Adjusted EBITDA growth for Bausch Health, excluding Bausch + Lomb, and exceeded its 2025 guidance on all metrics. Key strategic moves included the acquisition of Shibo's aesthetics distribution business in China and a $1.7 billion debt exchange offer.

feed.title: Deere Earnings Are Coming. Investors Are Looking for a Bottom in Farming.
feed.summary: Deere is set to report its fiscal first-quarter earnings on Thursday morning. The stock has seen a significant rise, up 30% since its last earnings report in November. Investors are keenly watching for insights into the health of the U.S. farming economy.

feed.title: Omnicom Announces $5 Billion Share Repurchase Program and Entry into $2.5 Billion of Accelerated Share Repurchase Arrangements
feed.summary: Omnicom (NYSE: OMC) has announced a significant capital return strategy, including a new $5 billion share repurchase program and the immediate execution of $2.5 billion in accelerated share repurchase (ASR) arrangements, funded by cash on hand. The initial delivery of ASR shares is expected by February 20, 2026, with final settlement by the end of Q2 2026. This move follows a period where the company's stock has faced muted or negative reactions despite positive strategic announcements and places the buyback near its 52-week low.

feed.title: Vir Biotechnology (NASDAQ:VIR) CAO Brent Sabatini Sells 1,530 Shares
feed.summary: Vir Biotechnology's CAO, Brent Sabatini, sold 1,530 shares of the company's stock on February 13th at an average price of $7.79, reducing his stake by 3.10%. Following the sale, he directly owns 47,872 shares valued at approximately $372,923. The stock traded up 4.2% to $7.82 midday, with a mixed but predominantly positive analyst outlook and an average target price of $16.29.

feed.title: Avis Budget Group Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year Results
feed.summary: Avis Budget Group reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2025, showing a challenging Q4 with a net loss including a significant EV impairment charge, but strategic moves for future growth. The company saw a slight decrease in Q4 revenues to $2.7 billion and a full-year revenue of $11.7 billion, while adjusted EBITDA improved for both periods. Avis Budget Group is focusing on fleet discipline, balance sheet strengthening, and customer experience to drive sustainable earnings growth moving into 2026.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (CAR) Avis Budget Group, Inc. Reports Q4 Revenue $2.66B, vs. FactSet Est of $2.74B
feed.summary: Avis Budget Group, Inc. (CAR) has reported its Q4 revenue at $2.66 billion, falling short of FactSet's estimate of $2.74 billion. Despite the revenue miss, the company's Q4 loss narrowed. Avis Budget Group provides mobility solutions through brands like Avis, Budget, and Zipcar worldwide.

feed.title: $1.50 per share: M&T Bank sets March 31 cash dividend payout
feed.summary: M&T Bank Corporation (NYSE: MTB) has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $1.50 per share on its common stock. The dividend will be payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders who are on record by the close of business on March 2, 2026. This declaration provides a clear return to shareholders with specified payment and record dates.

feed.title: Costamare rises to 11-year high after Q4 beat, adding charter agreements worth $940M (CMRE:NYSE)
feed.summary: Costamare (CMRE) shares rose to an 11-year high after the company reported better-than-expected Q4 adjusted earnings and revenues. The shipping company also announced new charter agreements valued at approximately $940 million. This performance positions Costamare for its best closing since August 2014.

feed.title: ECS Appoints Donnie Scott as President
feed.summary: ECS has appointed Donnie Scott as its new president, succeeding John Heneghan. Scott will lead ASGN’s federal segment, focusing on growth strategy, operational execution, and addressing complex government IT requirements. He brings over 25 years of leadership experience and a track record of driving growth and innovation in federal services and products.

feed.title: Coca-Cola works to expand digital footprint as consumer habits shift
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is focusing on expanding its digital footprint as consumer shopping habits rapidly change. The CEO views digital transformation as a "superpower" for the company. This initiative aims to address the competitive challenges posed by the evolving digital landscape.

feed.title: Sabre Earnings: Accelerating Air Volumes and AI Strategy Calm Investor Concerns
feed.summary: Sabre (SABR) reported strong fourth-quarter results with a 3.0% sales increase and improved EBITDA margins, driven by a 3.7% rise in air booking volumes. The company expects accelerating revenue growth in 2026, forecasting 5% air volume growth and leveraging its foundational technology for AI integration. Despite its potential in AI, shares are seen as undervalued, with an unchanged fair value estimate of $2.82 per share, and the company has also improved its liquidity profile by reducing debt.

feed.title: Ralph Lauren stock hits all-time high at 380.34 USD
feed.summary: Ralph Lauren (RL) stock has reached an all-time high of $380.34, currently trading at $380.75, which is slightly above its previous 52-week high. The company demonstrates strong financial health with impressive gross profit margins of 69.65% and a perfect Piotroski Score of 9. Despite a recent stock decline in premarket trading after exceeding Q3 2026 earnings and revenue forecasts, the surge reflects positive investor confidence and strategic initiatives.

feed.title: Tide thinks it's unlocked the future of the $25 billion laundry industry. Meet the evo tile, a waterless detergent pod
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble's Tide is launching Tide evo, a new waterless detergent square, which it believes will transform the $25 billion laundry care market. These palm-sized tiles are made of woven fibers that dissolve in cold water and come in recyclable packaging, offering a sustainable and cost-effective alternative to traditional detergents. The product, which took a decade to develop, builds on the success of Tide Pods and represents a significant new revenue stream for P&G.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Opened Accounts for Epstein Trusts as Late as 2019
feed.summary: Documents released by the U.S. Justice Department reveal that Morgan Stanley opened accounts for Jeffrey Epstein's trusts between 2015 and 2019, years after his 2008 sex offender conviction, even as other major Wall Street banks distanced themselves. One account opened in 2019 was closed shortly after, following a previous closure of another Epstein trust account in 2017 by the bank's risk officers. The emails also show that Epstein's longtime accountant, Richard Kahn, handled communications with the bank.

feed.title: Life sciences firm to invest $1 billion to build Montgomery County plant
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson announced a $1 billion investment to construct a new cell-therapy manufacturing facility at its campus in Lower Gwynedd Township, Montgomery County. This facility will create 500 manufacturing jobs and 4,000 construction jobs, supporting the production of medicines for cancer, immune, and neurological diseases. The project highlights Pennsylvania's growing life sciences industry and is backed by $41.5 million in state investment, further cementing the region as a leader in medical innovation.

feed.title: British Consumer Association Withdraws Competition Claim Against Qualcomm
feed.summary: A British consumers' organization, Which?, has withdrawn its competition claim against Qualcomm after concluding that the Competition Appeal Tribunal would likely find Qualcomm not at fault. The claim, filed in February 2021 on behalf of around 29 million British consumers, alleged that Qualcomm had breached UK competition law by charging inflated fees for technology licenses, which were then passed on to consumers as higher smartphone prices. Qualcomm welcomed the withdrawal, stating that it reaffirms judicial holdings that its licensing practices are lawful and do not harm competition.

feed.title: AI Pioneer Fei-Fei Li's World Labs Raises $1 Billion Funding Round
feed.summary: World Labs, an AI startup founded by Fei-Fei Li, has successfully raised $1 billion in a funding round, with Autodesk contributing $200 million. Other major investors include NVIDIA, AMD, and Andreessen Horowitz, among others. The company specializes in spatial intelligence through world models like its flagship product Marble, which generates 3D environments, and plans to use the funds to expand applications in robotics, scientific discovery, and creativity.

feed.title: Form 424B2 JPMORGAN CHASE & CO
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has filed a Form 424B2 for $699,000 Contingent Interest Notes due February 16, 2029, with payments linked to the lesser performing of the S&P 500® and Russell 2000® Indices. These notes offer contingent interest payments if both indices are at or above 75.00% of their initial value, but investors risk losing a significant portion or all of their principal if the final value of either index falls below its trigger value. Investing in these notes carries risks including potential loss of principal, no guaranteed interest payments, and credit risks of JPMorgan Financial and JPMorgan Chase & Co.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems shares pop on earnings beat, custom chip optimism
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems saw its shares rise over 6% after reporting better-than-expected earnings for Q4 2025, with an adjusted EPS of $1.99 and revenue of $1.44 billion. CEO Anirudh Devgan highlighted that nearly half of their business comes from "system companies" designing their own optimized chips, a trend he expects to accelerate, citing Apple and Google as key examples. The company also provided a strong revenue forecast for the next fiscal year, projecting $5.9 billion to $6 billion.

feed.title: Whittier Street Health Center to Honor State Street Chairman and CEO Ronald P. O’Hanley at Annual Fundraising Gala
feed.summary: Whittier Street Health Center will honor Ronald P. O’Hanley, Chairman and CEO of State Street, at its annual fundraising gala on April 9th at the Hilton Boston Park Plaza Hotel. The event, "A Toast of Ron O’Hanley," celebrates exceptional business leaders and philanthropists whose values align with Whittier’s mission of providing high-quality, accessible healthcare and social services to diverse communities, regardless of their financial status. O'Hanley is recognized for his leadership in connecting the private sector, government, and non-profits to support the well-being of the Greater Boston region.

feed.title: NRG Energy to expand power plant complex in northeast Harris County
feed.summary: NRG Energy plans to expand its Greens Bayou facility in northeast Harris County by building a new natural gas plant. This project aims to power approximately 110,000 homes annually and address Texas's increasing electricity demand due to economic development and population growth. The 455-megawatt facility received a $370 million loan from the Texas Energy Fund and qualified for property tax cuts under the Texas Jobs Energy, Technology, and Innovation program.

feed.title: Amazon.com Inc., Goldman Sachs share gains lead Dow's nearly 150-point jump
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by nearly 150 points, driven by significant share gains from Amazon.com Inc. and Goldman Sachs. These increases contributed to an overall positive movement in the index.

feed.title: Nationwide expands Ting program; AIG completes $2.7B acquisitions: Insurtech news
feed.summary: Nationwide is expanding its Ting program to 500,000 additional homes, offering free smart sensors for fire prevention. AIG has completed strategic acquisitions of stakes in Convex Group and Onex Corporation totaling $2.7 billion. The article also covers various appointments, new partnerships, and product launches within the insurtech industry.

feed.title: Texas sues Dow, Braskem over ‘habitual’ pellet pollution
feed.summary: Texas state officials have filed a lawsuit against Dow and Braskem, alleging "habitual" plastic pellet pollution from their petrochemical complex located in Seadrift, Texas. The lawsuit targets the two companies over environmental concerns related to their operations. The article was published on February 18, 2026.
